extraction.one_line_summary: HODGE Alvaro won via late leadout after Quick-Step stayed organized while Bennett's Deceuninck train disintegrated in messy final 2km.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step pivoted from GC protection (Wellens) to opportunistic sprint support for Hodge when leadout trains fragmented.
Bennett isolated despite red jersey; Deceuninck train collapsed early leaving him trapped barrier-side with 1100m remaining.
Trek-Segafredo committed to Teuns despite no dedicated leadout, exposing vulnerability when multiple teams lacked clear sprint hierarchy.
Sunweb indecision between Herschey/Nowhaus left both without support, revealing poor pre-stage role definition despite GC position.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: trapped on barriers at 1100m out after leadout disintegrated, suggesting team coordination issues despite stage 4 win
rider_signals.HODGE Alvaro: positive: capitalized when Quick-Step unexpectedly supported him over GC priorities, showing team flexibility pays
rider_signals.DEMAR Arnaud: neutral: solo positioning strategy failed when forced to follow others after teammates shed early
rider_signals.HERSCHEY Mark: negative: team couldn't decide between him and Nowhaus for sprint despite lying 2nd GC at 4s
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: neutral: Trek committed resources but no proper train suggests emergency sprint option rather than genuine threat
